description: Manages stateful FIX protocol sessions including logon/logout lifecycle, heartbeat monitoring, sequence number integrity, and time-window enforcement

actors.name: Session Initiator
actors.description: Client-side participant that initiates outbound FIX connections and sends Logon

actors.name: Session Acceptor
actors.description: Server-side participant that listens for incoming FIX connections and responds to Logon

actors.name: Application Layer
actors.description: Business application that implements callbacks for session lifecycle events and message handling

values.name: disconnected
values.description: No TCP connection; session is not active

values.name: logged_on
values.description: FIX session fully established; both sides exchanged Logon messages

transitions.description: Logon exchange complete; both sides confirmed
Received and responded to valid Logon request
Application or operator initiates graceful logout
Logout response received or logout timeout elapsed
Heartbeat timeout or network error forces disconnect

session_identity: Both SenderCompID and TargetCompID must match configured session identity on every received message
A SessionQualifier can be added to distinguish parallel sessions between the same counterparties
sequence_numbers: All messages are numbered sequentially starting at 1; gaps trigger a ResendRequest
Out-of-sequence messages are queued until a Resend fills the gap
ResetSeqNumFlag=Y in a Logon message causes both parties to reset to sequence number 1
Application may manually set next sender or target sequence number before logon
heartbeat: Heartbeat messages are sent at the configured interval when no other messages are sent
If no message is received within heartbeat_interval seconds, a TestRequest is sent
If no response to TestRequest arrives within heartbeat_interval, the session disconnects
Heartbeat interval is negotiated during Logon (sent by initiator, may be overridden by acceptor)
logon_time_window: Sessions can be restricted to a time window (e.g., market hours) via StartTime/EndTime
A session received outside its valid logon window is immediately disconnected
NonStopSession=Y disables time-window enforcement for 24/7 operation

session_recovery.ResetOnLogon: reset sequence numbers each time a logon occurs

session_recovery.ResetOnLogout: reset when a logout is processed

session_recovery.ResetOnDisconnect: reset whenever the TCP connection drops

session_disabled.result: Logon request is ignored; disconnect is triggered

given: current time is outside configured logon time window
application throws RejectLogon exception in fromAdmin callback
SenderCompID or TargetCompID does not match configured session values
comp_id_mismatch.result: Message rejected with SessionReject; may force logout

given: no logon response received within logon_timeout seconds
logon_timeout_elapsed.result: Session disconnected; initiator will attempt reconnect after interval

given: no message received for heartbeat_interval seconds
TestRequest was sent but no response after heartbeat_interval more seconds
heartbeat_timeout.result: Session terminated due to heartbeat timeout

given: message received with sequence number higher than expected
sequence_gap_detected.result: ResendRequest sent for the missing range; message queued until gap filled

given: current time is within logon time window
Logon message contains valid SenderCompID and TargetCompID
logon_successful.result: Session is fully established; application receives onLogon callback

given: Logout message received or application calls logout()
logout_successful.result: Session cleanly terminated; application receives onLogout callback

logon_flow.description: FIX session establishment between initiator and acceptor

steps.action: Send Logon message with HeartBtInt, EncryptMethod, and optional ResetSeqNumFlag
Validate SenderCompID/TargetCompID, check logon time window, call application.fromAdmin()
Receive Logon response; call application.onLogon(); session is now active
Call application.onLogon(); session is now active

heartbeat_flow.description: Keepalive mechanism to detect dead connections

steps.action: Track time since last sent/received message
Send Heartbeat if heartbeat_interval elapsed since last outgoing message
If heartbeat_interval elapsed since last incoming message, send TestRequest
If TestRequest response not received within heartbeat_interval, disconnect

errors.message: Session is not enabled for logon
Received logon outside of valid logon time
Timed out waiting for logon response

events.name: fix.session.logged_on
events.description: FIX session successfully established after Logon exchange

events.name: fix.session.logged_out
events.description: Session cleanly terminated via Logout exchange

events.name: fix.session.disconnected
events.description: Session terminated due to error, timeout, or network failure

events.name: fix.session.resend_requested
events.description: Sequence gap detected; ResendRequest sent for missing messages

events.name: fix.session.message_rejected
events.description: A message was rejected due to validation failure

related.reason: Session sends and receives FIX messages structured by the message-building feature
Session requires an active TCP connection managed by the connection layer
All sent and received messages are persisted for sequence number recovery
Incoming messages are validated against a DataDictionary before delivery
